description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg, finding F2 [High, performance; held at High on the unbounded-growth ratchet]. Fix before wide pre-push hook adoption — pre-merge preferred.

Two parts:
(a) Hot-path waste: Evidence.Sync.run_attempt eagerly loads+decodes EVERY entry on both refs (git cat-file per entry, both sides ~= 2-4N subprocesses) before reconcile, even when local==remote and the outcome is :noop — the case the pre-push hook hits on nearly every push. Short-circuit on equal commit SHAs makes the common path O(1) regardless of store size. Rebuild path is also batchable (git mktree from stdin vs N temp files + hash-object + update-index).
(b) Unbounded ratchet: one entry per verified tree state (~2k within months for an active team; ~30-60s projected on the pre-push path). mix spec.prune already has the right reachability-based retention but is manual — fold its keep-set into sync periodically or on a size threshold. Misses fail safe by design (delta_available?: false fallback; evidence never gates), so retention costs nothing.

Files: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex:70-79,177-194,214-258; priv/hooks/pre-push; lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W3)
Note: specled_-737 (shared Evidence.Git) and specled_-7xz (sync quarantine tolerance: entries/2 tagged {:known,entry}|{:raw,content} representation, warnings field on Sync.run/2 results) have landed on the epic branch — line numbers in the ticket body predate both. Build on the current epic-branch shape.
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex (only if folding retention into sync requires task-side wiring)
- priv/hooks/pre-push (only if the short-circuit needs hook-side changes)
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/store.ex (only if the size-threshold trigger needs store cooperation)
- test/specled_ex/evidence/**, test/mix/tasks/**
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (spec the short-circuit and retention behavior)
Out of scope (intent): part (a) O(1) no-op short-circuit on equal commit SHAs before any entry loading; batchable rebuild is optional if reached. Part (b) fold spec.prune's reachability-based keep-set into sync periodically or on a size threshold (auto-prune). Preserve: evidence never gates; misses fail safe (delta_available?: false fallback); quarantine tolerance from specled_-7xz must survive (do not regress its warnings/carry-through behavior). Any file outside these globs must be disclosed in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Commit: a70ed41
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex, test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, .spec/decisions/specled.decision.sync_noop_short_circuit_and_auto_prune.md (new). Also touched .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md — outside this ticket's Allowed touches, disclosed below.
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest "a no-op sync (local and remote already equal) never re-reads an entry, even a quarantined one"; "a real reconciliation folds the reachable-set keep-set in once the merged entry count crosses the threshold"; "a real reconciliation below the threshold leaves unreachable entries for explicit spec.prune". Also fixed sync_fixture's bare-origin init (added `-b main`) — tests run with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null so init.defaultBranch isn't honored, and the bare origin's default HEAD (master) didn't match the seed repo's pushed branch (main), leaving clones with an unborn HEAD; the two new tests needed a real checked-out tree hash. Test-fixture-only change, no other test's behavior affected.
Verification stubs flipped: none flipped false->true; added specled.evidence_store.sync_auto_prune and specled.evidence_store.sync_noop_short_circuit to the existing execute:true command-kind stub (mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs).
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/ test/mix/tasks/ (99 tests, 0 failures); also ran the full suite as this touches shared plumbing (mix test: 1001 tests + 3 properties, 0 failures); mix spec.check --base HEAD with commands executing (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, branch findings=0); mix format --check-formatted and mix deps.unlock --check-unused both clean.
Final-tier runs: 1
Audit-expanded scope: none (iteration 1)
Deferred must requirements: none — both ticket parts (O(1) no-op short circuit; threshold-based auto-prune folding) implemented and covered.

Disclosed out-of-scope touch: .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md. mix spec.check's branch guard flagged lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex (changed to delegate to the new Sync.reachable_keep_set/1) as impacting subject specled.mix_tasks. Rather than leave that as a silent info-level finding, I lightly reworded specled.tasks.prune_evidence to note the shared function it now calls (no behavior, priority, or modal-verb change) and added the new decision id to its decisions: list.

Summary: Sync.run_attempt short-circuits to O(1) when the fetched remote commit already equals the local spec-evidence commit and no explicit :keep was supplied — no ls-tree, cat-file, or decode runs on either ref, covering the pre-push hook's near-universal no-drift outcome. reconcile_entries folds mix spec.prune's reachability keep-set into a real (non-no-op) reconciliation once the merged entry count crosses @auto_prune_entry_threshold (500, overridable via :auto_prune_threshold), reusing the same reachable_keep_set query mix spec.prune now calls too (deduplicated out of a private copy in spec.prune.ex). A failed keep-set computation degrades to a plain unpruned sync rather than failing the attempt — auto-prune is housekeeping, not a correctness gate. Added two new must requirements (sync_auto_prune, sync_noop_short_circuit) and narrowed prune_explicit_only from "sync shall never prune" to "deletion only happens via this one shared reachable-set computation, explicit or threshold-triggered" (still must), recorded in ADR specled.decision.sync_noop_short_circuit_and_auto_prune, which explicitly reconciles with evidence_orphan_branch_split's earlier rejection of unconditional "prune-on-sync" (that rejected pruning on every push; this is threshold-gated and reachability-scoped, identical semantics to explicit prune).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 6 (sync.ex, spec.prune.ex, sync_test.exs, evidence_store.spec.md, mix_tasks.spec.md, new ADR)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 2
  - .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md — DISCLOSED. Forcing reason VALID: spec.prune.ex now delegates to Sync.reachable_keep_set/1, so the branch guard flags impl for subject specled.mix_tasks; updating the covering spec is the designed branch-guard-forced co-update, not opportunistic. spec.check --base 99add6f raises 0 findings on it.
  - .spec/decisions/specled.decision.sync_noop_short_circuit_and_auto_prune.md (new ADR) — DISCLOSED (listed under Files touched). Forcing reason VALID: narrowing the prune_explicit_only must-requirement (removing the "sync shall never prune" negative assertion) requires a weakening-set ADR or AppendOnly errors; adds-exception is in the weakening set and the ADR's affects lists prune_explicit_only. Intrinsic to the "spec the retention behavior" allowance. Not enumerated in Allowed-touches but load-bearing and forced.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (ticket Out-of-Scope was intent-level — optional batchable rebuild — not glob-based; no forbidden file touched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: spec.check --base 99add6f with commands executing (status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, branch findings=0); both new scenarios (sync_auto_prune, sync_noop_short_circuit) added to the execute:true command stub + tagged_tests coverage, both passing.
- Requirements coverage:
  - Part (a) O(1) no-op short-circuit: covered by test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs:188 ("a no-op sync ... never re-reads an entry, even a quarantined one"). LOAD-BEARING: if the short-circuit is removed, the 2nd run re-reads the tree, re-quarantines notes.txt, and noop.warnings becomes [quarantine_warning] instead of [], failing the `noop.warnings == []` assertion. Traced run_attempt: SHA equality (local_commit==remote_commit) is checked at sync.ex:131 AFTER only fetch+rev-parse and BEFORE reconcile_attempt/entries() — no ls-tree/cat-file/decode on either ref. Seams: sleep fn (test injects no-op).
  - :keep bypass of short-circuit (explicit prune on up-to-date store): covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs:16 (UNTOUCHED). LOAD-BEARING: it runs Sync.run (pushes 3 entries, making refs equal), then Spec.Prune.run which fetches (local==remote) and calls Sync.run(keep: mapset). The `not is_struct(keep, MapSet)` guard at sync.ex:131 is false so it falls to reconcile_attempt -> reconcile/6 local==local clause (sync.ex:160) which prunes because keep is a MapSet. If the guard were dropped, prune would no-op and the unreachable entry would survive, failing line 39. Confirms explicit prune with equal refs still works.
  - Part (b) auto-prune over threshold: covered by sync_test.exs:203. Fails if auto-prune disabled (unreachable "fff" entry retained instead of dropped).
  - Auto-prune under threshold: covered by sync_test.exs:224. Fails if threshold comparison inverted (would prune when it must not).
  - spec.prune delegation unchanged: spec_prune_task_test.exs untouched and passing; spec.prune.ex now calls Sync.reachable_keep_set/1 (dedup of the deleted private reachable_tree_hashes/1), same keep: contract to Sync.run.
- Spec-semantics assessment:
  - prune_explicit_only narrowing is AUTHORIZED: adds-exception is in AppendOnly's weakening set (deprecates|weakens|narrows-scope|adds-exception), the ADR's affects lists specled.evidence_store.prune_explicit_only, and spec.check --base 99add6f returns findings=0. The clean pass is itself proof the ADR is load-bearing: negative_removed/must_downgraded fire on removing "sync shall never prune" UNLESS a weakening-set ADR targets the id.
  - Narrowing is SOUND: new text confines deletion to "one shared reachable-set computation ... never by any other deletion path." The reachable-set computation only drops entries not reachable from any live branch head / remote-tracking ref — reachability-scoped, identical to explicit prune. Still forbids unbounded/arbitrary silent deletion; permits only the threshold-gated reachability prune. The review finding F2(b) explicitly asked for exactly this fold-in.
  - ADR reconciles with evidence_orphan_branch_split (does NOT contradict): orphan_branch_split rejected UNCONDITIONAL prune-on-sync ("turns every push destructive, non-monotonic reads mid-review"). The new ADR is threshold-gated (fires only on real reconciliations crossing @auto_prune_entry_threshold=500) and reachability-scoped, so a reachable/live entry is never a pruning candidate and the mid-review non-monotonic concern doesn't apply. The ADR names and addresses that prior rejection explicitly.
  - mix_tasks prune_evidence reword: softens "explicit-only command" -> "explicit command" and adds a delegation clause naming reachable_keep_set/1. This is a semantic softening (not pure rename), but it is ADR-authorized (ADR affects lists specled.tasks.prune_evidence; decisions list updated), faithful to code (spec.prune.ex does delegate), and raises 0 spec.check findings. No added obligation/prohibition the code fails to satisfy, so the stale-spec lesson is not violated. The IMPLEMENTED note's "no behavior change" is accurate for spec.prune runtime behavior but understates the "-only" softening — advisory only.
- Quarantine regression ({:raw,content} handling under auto-prune): keep_entries filters by Path.rootname regardless of {:known}/{:raw}, so a quarantined entry is KEPT when its tree-hash path is reachable and PRUNED when unreachable — same treatment as known entries, not unconditional drop and not exempt (the reasonable behavior). No regression of specled_-7xz tolerance: below threshold everything is carried through byte-identical with warnings (unchanged), and the no-op path reads nothing. Pinned indirectly: sync_test.exs:188 proves a quarantined entry is carried through with warnings==1 on a real push. Minor gap (advisory): no dedicated test pins the quarantined+unreachable+over-threshold prune case (the over-threshold test uses known entries) — behavior is correct by construction.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Optional batchable rebuild (git mktree) not attempted — explicitly optional in the ticket.
- Targeted verification: PASS. `mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/ test/mix/tasks/` = 99 tests, 0 failures. `mix test --stale` = 1001 tests + 3 properties, 0 failures. `mix spec.check --base 99add6f` = status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0, branch findings=0. `mix spec.check --base HEAD` = pass. (A transient "/bin/sh: <temp>.sh: No such file or directory" appeared during the targeted run — pre-existing command-stub temp-file race, benign, 0 failures.)
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design; --stale happened to cover the full suite here as shared plumbing changed).
- Audit-expanded scope: none.

Fixture change: sync_fixture's bare-origin init now passes `-b main`. SETUP-ONLY: with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null the bare origin defaulted HEAD to master while the seed pushed main, leaving clones with an unborn HEAD; the new auto-prune tests need a resolvable `rev-parse HEAD^{tree}` on the clone. It makes the fixture well-formed and does not alter any existing assertion's semantics (existing tests use synthetic hash() tree hashes and don't hit reachable_keep_set); all 1001 tests pass.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Advisory: add a test pinning quarantined-entry pruning when unreachable AND over threshold to close the one uncovered corner of the {:raw} + auto-prune interaction.
- Advisory: IMPLEMENTED's characterization of the mix_tasks reword as "no behavior change" understates the "explicit-only"->"explicit" softening; the change is fine (ADR-authorized, faithful, 0 findings) but the disclosure could be more precise.

AUDIT: APPROVE

Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Reasoning: All three new/modified musts have load-bearing, falsifiable tests, and every named seam traces to a real call site.
- sync_noop_short_circuit: sync.ex:131 checks SHA-equality AND `not is_struct(keep, MapSet)` before any entries() read; covered by sync_test.exs:188 — the 2nd run over a tree holding an injected raw/quarantined `notes.txt` asserts `noop.warnings == []`, which only holds if no re-read/re-quarantine occurred (falsifiable: dropping the short-circuit routes to reconcile_attempt->entries() at sync.ex:140-141, re-emitting the quarantine warning).
- sync_auto_prune: resolve_keep (sync.ex:189-206) only prunes when keep is nil AND map_size(merged) > threshold, and on reachable_keep_set {:error} degrades to nil (keep_entries/2 nil-clause at sync.ex:352 keeps all) — evidence never gates. Covered over-threshold at sync_test.exs:203 and under-threshold at sync_test.exs:224.
- Seam (a) pruned tree actually lands: reconcile_entries (sync.ex:172-182) writes the *keep-filtered* merged_entries via write_tree->commit_tree->update_local_ref->{:push}; the over-threshold test's assert `evidence_ids(fixture.a) == [reachable]` reads `refs/heads/spec-evidence` after push (sync_test.exs evidence_ids), so it pins the pushed ref state, not an in-memory list.
- Seam (b) :keep bypass: the guard at sync.ex:131 sends any MapSet keep to reconcile_attempt even on equal SHAs; reconcile/6 equal-SHA clause (sync.ex:160) then prunes because keep is a MapSet. Pinned by UNTOUCHED spec_prune_task_test.exs:16 — Prune.run fetches an up-to-date store (local==remote) and line 39 asserts the unreachable entry is dropped; if the short-circuit swallowed prune it would :noop and line 39 would fail.
- Seam (c) keep-set failure: {:error} -> nil -> unpruned merge; cannot become a sync failure. Confirmed.

Scope: diff touches exactly 6 files (verifier's count is correct); 2 outside Allowed globs (mix_tasks.spec.md, new ADR), both disclosed and genuinely forced — the branch guard flags the allowed spec.prune.ex delegation change onto subject specled.mix_tasks, and speccing retention (an allowed edit to evidence_store.spec.md that adds sync_auto_prune) directly contradicts the old "sync shall never prune" clause, so a weakening-set ADR is intrinsic, not opportunistic. Both edits are minimal (one requirement each + decisions-list entry).

Weakening authorized and honestly reconciled: append_only.spec.md:46-47/57-58/81-88 puts `adds-exception` in the weakening set; the new ADR's change_type is adds-exception, affects lists prune_explicit_only with non-empty reverses_what, so removing the negative "sync shall never prune" assertion clears negative_removed; priority stays `must` (no must_downgraded); the covering scenario was updated not deleted (no scenario_regression). The narrowed requirement still confines deletion to the reachability-scoped keep-set ("never by any other deletion path"), so reachable entries are never droppable — no unbounded/arbitrary deletion. The ADR genuinely differs from evidence_orphan_branch_split's rejected "prune-on-sync" (that was unconditional/every-push destructive; this is threshold-gated at 500 + reachability-scoped, so mid-review reachable entries are always kept), and it names and addresses that prior rejection rather than merely asserting compatibility. Quarantine tolerance from specled_-7xz is not regressed: keep_entries filters by path regardless of {:known}/{:raw}, below-threshold carries all entries byte-identical, and the no-op path reads nothing.

I concur with the verifier's two advisories (no dedicated test for quarantined+unreachable+over-threshold prune; IMPLEMENTED understates the "explicit-only"->"explicit" softening) — both are advisory, correct-by-construction, and not grounds to bounce.

title: review-F2: evidence sync loads every entry even on no-op; store unbounded without auto-prune
